danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies with visible weapons (pistols, machine guns, grenades, scythes, boomerangs, knives)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; player must engage or evade
Global-scale danger; mission stakes are existential

danger_signals.visual_form: Scorpions and dogs as enemy encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats in addition to armed foes

danger_signals.visual_form: Horizontally scrolling stages and stationary combat screens with time pressure
danger_signals.meaning: Urgency and risk of mission failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Dr. Barrows held captive narrative element

safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for sustaining combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Infrared scope acquisition in maze sections
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage gains; enhanced situational awareness

safety_signals.visual_form: Agent James and Agent Sylvia provide information and weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Allied faction assistance; trusted information source

safety_signals.visual_form: Varied screen types (scrolling, stationary, 3D, maze, sniper)
safety_signals.meaning: Adaptability and skill variability reduce monotonic threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Arm of Mafat (leadership), regular Mafat operatives with diverse weapons, Bodyguard (Ahmad Khan's protection), Canine (base guardian)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mafat hideout, Mafat base locations; satellite capture system control center

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Agents in Ferrari vehicles (treated as hostile in car chase sequences)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Agent James and Agent Sylvia as support contacts; player alliance

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: FSB agents in Ferrari vehicles during chase sequences

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Professional assassin/agent; operates independently; trusted by CIA

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mission objective briefing; narrative context for all level designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemy types and obstacles serve the Mafat extortion plot; urgency in timed sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Both CIA and FSB agents appear as hostile in Ferrari sequences despite different allegiances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must engage vehicles from both factions; world-state of geopolitical tension reflected in combat encounters

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dr. Barrows held hostage in Paris
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescue objective narrative; locationally significant (Paris setting)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must progress through levels to locate and free hostage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Satellite Capture System as a target location/objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final objective: destruction of control infrastructure

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct screen types (scrolling combat, stationary melee, Ferrari chase, maze exploration, sniper operation) communicate different mission phases: combat infiltration → tactical engagement → vehicular escape → base exploration → precision assassination
Pistols, machine guns, grenades, scythes, boomerangs, knives as visual indicators of opponent capability and tactical approach required
Paris, London, specific hideouts and bases anchor the geopolitical narrative visually
Infrared scope availability signals transition into darker/more hazardous environments requiring night vision advantage

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary combat encounter; protected position within hideout
Stationary melee combat; dedicated to target protection
Stationary combat; awaits player at Mafat base
Horizontally scrolling pursuit; diverse weaponry indicates varied threat levels
High-speed vehicular combat; grenade throwing indicates ranged threat
Information provision and weapon supply; non-hostile

notes: This is a Cold War-era espionage game that communicates political tension through dual-faction hostility (CIA and FSB both appear as enemies despite different geopolitical alignment). The visual manifestation of world-state relies heavily on narrative framing rather than dynamic environmental changes. Danger signals are primarily enemy-centric (weapons, creatures) rather than environmental hazards. The game's multiple screen types serve both mechanical variety and narrative communication of mission progression stages.
